\"\"\"\n        Follow memcached's convention where a timeout greater than 30 days is\n        treated as an absolute expiration timestamp instead of a relative\n        offset (#12399).\n        \"\"\"\n        cache.set(\"key1\", \"eggs\", 60 * 60 * 24 * 30 + 1)  # 30 days + 1 second\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key1\"), \"eggs\")\n\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"key2\", \"ham\", 60 * 60 * 24 * 30 + 1), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key2\"), \"ham\")\n\n        cache.set_many(\n            {\"key3\": \"sausage\", \"key4\": \"lobster bisque\"}, 60 * 60 * 24 * 30 + 1\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key3\"), \"sausage\")\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key4\"), \"lobster bisque\")\n\n    @retry()\n    def test_forever_timeout(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Passing in None into timeout results in a value that is cached forever\n        \"\"\"\n        cache.set(\"key1\", \"eggs\", None)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key1\"), \"eggs\")\n\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"key2\", \"ham\", None), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key2\"), \"ham\")\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"key1\", \"new eggs\", None), False)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key1\"), \"eggs\")\n\n        cache.set_many({\"key3\": \"sausage\", \"key4\": \"lobster bisque\"}, None)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key3\"), \"sausage\")\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key4\"), \"lobster bisque\")\n\n        cache.set(\"key5\", \"belgian fries\", timeout=1)\n        self.assertIs(cache.touch(\"key5\", timeout=None), True)\n        time.sleep(2)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key5\"), \"belgian fries\")\n\n    def test_zero_timeout(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Passing in zero into timeout results in a value that is not cached\n        \"\"\"\n        cache.set(\"key1\", \"eggs\", 0)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"key1\"))\n\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"key2\", \"ham\", 0), True)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"key2\"))\n\n        cache.set_many({\"key3\": \"sausage\", \"key4\": \"lobster bisque\"}, 0)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"key3\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"key4\"))\n\n        cache.set(\"key5\", \"belgian fries\", timeout=5)\n        self.assertIs(cache.touch(\"key5\", timeout=0), True)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"key5\"))\n\n    def test_float_timeout(self):\n        # Make sure a timeout given as a float doesn't crash anything.\n        cache.set(\"key1\", \"spam\", 100.2)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"key1\"), \"spam\")\n\n    def _perform_cull_test(self, cull_cache_name, initial_count, final_count):\n        try:\n            cull_cache = caches[cull_cache_name]\n        except InvalidCacheBackendError:\n            self.skipTest(\"Culling isn't implemented.\")\n\n        # Create initial cache key entries. This will overflow the cache,\n        # causing a cull.\n        for i in range(1, initial_count):\n            cull_cache.set(\"cull%d\" % i, \"value\", 1000)\n        count = 0\n        # Count how many keys are left in the cache.\n        for i in range(1, initial_count):\n            if cull_cache.has_key(\"cull%d\" % i):\n                count += 1\n        self.assertEqual(count, final_count)\n\n    def test_cull(self):\n        self._perform_cull_test(\"cull\", 50, 29)\n\n    def test_zero_cull(self):\n        self._perform_cull_test(\"zero_cull\", 50, 19)\n\n    def test_cull_delete_when_store_empty(self):\n        try:\n            cull_cache = caches[\"cull\"]\n        except InvalidCacheBackendError:\n            self.skipTest(\"Culling isn't implemented.\")\n        old_max_entries = cull_cache._max_entries\n        # Force _cull to delete on first cached record.\n        cull_cache._max_entries = -1\n        try:\n            cull_cache.set(\"force_cull_delete\", \"value\", 1000)\n            self.assertIs(cull_cache.has_key(\"force_cull_delete\"), True)\n        finally:\n            cull_cache._max_entries = old_max_entries\n\n    def _perform_invalid_key_test(self, key, expected_warning, key_func=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        All the builtin backends should warn (except memcached that should\n        error) on keys that would be refused by memcached. This encourages\n        portable caching code without making it too difficult to use production\n        backends with more liberal key rules. Refs #6447.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        # mimic custom ``make_key`` method being defined since the default will\n        # never show the below warnings\n        def func(key, *args):\n            return key\n\n        old_func = cache.key_func\n        cache.key_func = key_func or func\n\n        tests = [\n            (\"add\", [key, 1]),\n            (\"get\", [key]),\n            (\"set\", [key, 1]),\n            (\"incr\", [key]),\n            (\"decr\", [key]),\n            (\"touch\", [key]),\n            (\"delete\", [key]),\n            (\"get_many\", [[key, \"b\"]]),\n            (\"set_many\", [{key: 1, \"b\": 2}]),\n            (\"delete_many\", [[key, \"b\"]]),\n        ]\n        try:\n            for operation, args in tests:\n                with self.subTest(operation=operation):\n                    with self.assertWarns(CacheKeyWarning) as cm:\n                        getattr(cache, operation)(*args)\n                    self.assertEqual(str(cm.warning), expected_warning)\n        finally:\n            cache.key_func = old_func\n\n    def test_invalid_key_characters(self):\n        # memcached doesn't allow whitespace or control characters in keys.\n        key = \"key with spaces and 清\"\n        self._perform_invalid_key_test(key, KEY_ERRORS_WITH_MEMCACHED_MSG % key)\n\n    def test_invalid_key_length(self):\n        # memcached limits key length to 250.\n        key = (\"a\" * 250) + \"清\"\n        expected_warning = (\n            \"Cache key will cause errors if used with memcached: \"\n            \"%r (longer than %s)\" % (key, 250)\n        )\n        self._perform_invalid_key_test(key, expected_warning)\n\n    def test_invalid_with_version_key_length(self):\n        # Custom make_key() that adds a version to the key and exceeds the\n        # limit.\n        def key_func(key, *args):\n            return key + \":1\"\n\n        key = \"a\" * 249\n        expected_warning = (\n            \"Cache key will cause errors if used with memcached: \"\n            \"%r (longer than %s)\" % (key_func(key), 250)\n        )\n        self._perform_invalid_key_test(key, expected_warning, key_func=key_func)\n\n    def test_cache_versioning_get_set(self):\n        # set, using default version = 1\n        cache.set(\"answer1\", 42)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\"), 42)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=2))\n\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer1\"))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer1\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer1\", version=2))\n\n        # set, default version = 1, but manually override version = 2\n        cache.set(\"answer2\", 42, version=2)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer2\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\"), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n\n        # v2 set, using default version = 2\n        caches[\"v2\"].set(\"answer3\", 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer3\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer3\"), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer3\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer3\", version=2), 42)\n\n        # v2 set, default version = 2, but manually override version = 1\n        caches[\"v2\"].set(\"answer4\", 42, version=1)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer4\"), 42)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer4\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer4\", version=2))\n\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer4\"))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer4\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer4\", version=2))\n\n    def test_cache_versioning_add(self):\n        # add, default version = 1, but manually override version = 2\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"answer1\", 42, version=2), True)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"answer1\", 37, version=2), False)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertIs(cache.add(\"answer1\", 37, version=1), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=1), 37)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=2), 42)\n\n        # v2 add, using default version = 2\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer2\", 42), True)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer2\", 37), False)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer2\", 37, version=1), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=1), 37)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n\n        # v2 add, default version = 2, but manually override version = 1\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer3\", 42, version=1), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=2))\n\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer3\", 37, version=1), False)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=2))\n\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].add(\"answer3\", 37), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=1), 42)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer3\", version=2), 37)\n\n    def test_cache_versioning_has_key(self):\n        cache.set(\"answer1\", 42)\n\n        # has_key\n        self.assertIs(cache.has_key(\"answer1\"), True)\n        self.assertIs(cache.has_key(\"answer1\", version=1), True)\n        self.assertIs(cache.has_key(\"answer1\", version=2), False)\n\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].has_key(\"answer1\"), False)\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].has_key(\"answer1\", version=1), True)\n        self.assertIs(caches[\"v2\"].has_key(\"answer1\", version=2), False)\n\n    def test_cache_versioning_delete(self):\n        cache.set(\"answer1\", 37, version=1)\n        cache.set(\"answer1\", 42, version=2)\n        self.assertIs(cache.delete(\"answer1\"), True)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer1\", version=2), 42)\n\n        cache.set(\"answer2\", 37, version=1)\n        cache.set(\"answer2\", 42, version=2)\n        self.assertIs(cache.delete(\"answer2\", version=2), True)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=1), 37)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer2\", version=2))\n\n        cache.set(\"answer3\", 37, version=1)\n        cache.set(\"answer3\", 42, version=2)\n self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer\", version=2), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\", version=3))\n\n        self.assertEqual(cache.incr_version(\"answer\", version=2), 3)\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\", version=1))\n        self.assertIsNone(cache.get(\"answer\", version=2))\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"answer\", version=3), 42)\n\n        caches[\"v2\"].set(\"answer2\", 42)\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\"), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=2), 42)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=3))\n\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].incr_version(\"answer2\"), 3)\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\"))\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=1))\n        self.assertIsNone(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=2))\n        self.assertEqual(caches[\"v2\"].get(\"answer2\", version=3), 42)\n\n        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):\n            cache.incr_version(\"does_not_exist\")\n\n        cache.set(\"null\", None)\n        self.assertEqual(cache.incr_version(\"null\"), 2)\n\n    def test_decr_version(self):\n async def test_get_many_async_uses_specialized_implementation(self):\n        if (\n            cache.get_many.__func__ is not BaseCache.get_many\n            and cache.aget_many.__func__ is BaseCache.aget_many\n        ):\n            with mock.patch.object(cache, \"get_many\") as mocked:\n                mocked.__func__ = lambda x: x\n                await cache.aget_many({})\n                mocked.assert_called_once()\n\n    async def test_set_many_async_uses_specialized_implementation(self):\n        if (\n            cache.set_many.__func__ is not BaseCache.set_many\n cache.set_many({\"a\": 1, \"b\": 2})\n        cache.set(\"expired\", \"expired\", 0.01)\n        with self.assertNumQueries(1):\n            self.assertEqual(cache.get_many([\"a\", \"b\"]), {\"a\": 1, \"b\": 2})\n        time.sleep(0.02)\n        with self.assertNumQueries(2):\n            self.assertEqual(cache.get_many([\"a\", \"b\", \"expired\"]), {\"a\": 1, \"b\": 2})\n\n    def test_cull_queries(self):\n        old_max_entries = cache._max_entries\n        # Force _cull to delete on first cached record.\n        cache._max_entries = -1\n        with CaptureQueriesContext(connection) as captured_queries:\n            try:\n                cache.set(\"force_cull\", \"value\", 1000)\n            finally:\n                cache._max_entries = old_max_entries\n        num_count_queries = sum(\"COUNT\" in query[\"sql\"] for query in captured_queries)\n        self.assertEqual(num_count_queries, 1)\n        # Column names are quoted.\n        for query in captured_queries:\n            sql = query[\"sql\"]\n            if \"expires\" in sql:\n                self.assertIn(connection.ops.quote_name(\"expires\"), sql)\n            if \"cache_key\" in sql:\n                self.assertIn(connection.ops.quote_name(\"cache_key\"), sql)\n\n    def test_delete_cursor_rowcount(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The rowcount attribute should not be checked on a closed cursor.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        class MockedCursorWrapper(CursorWrapper):\n            is_closed = False\n\n            def close(self):\n                self.cursor.close()\n                self.is_closed = True\n\n            @property\n            def rowcount(self):\n                if self.is_closed:\n                    raise Exception(\"Cursor           self.assertIs(cache.delete(\"a\"), True)\n\n    def test_zero_cull(self):\n        self._perform_cull_test(\"zero_cull\", 50, 18)\n\n    def test_second_call_doesnt_crash(self):\n        out test_memcached_deletes_key_on_failed_set(self):\n        # By default memcached allows objects up to 1MB. For the cache_db\n        # session backend to always use the current session, memcached needs to\n        # delete the old key if it fails to set.\n        max_value_length = 2**20\n\n        cache.set(\"small_value\", \"a\")\n        self.assertEqual(cache.get(\"small_value\"), \"a\")\n\n        large_value = \"a\" * (max_value_length + 1)\n        try:\n            cache.set(\"small_value\", large_value)\n        except Exception:\n            # Most clients (e.g. pymemcache or pylibmc) raise when the value is\n            # too large. This test is primarily checking that the key was\n            # deleted, so the return/exception behavior for the set() itself is\n            # not important.\n            pass\n        # small_value should be deleted, or set if configured to accept larger\n        # values\n        value = cache.get(\"small_value\")\n        self.assertTrue(value is None or value == large_value)\n\n    def test_close(self):\n        # For clients that don't manage their connections properly, the\n        # connection is closed when the request is complete.\n        signals.request_finished.disconnect(close_old_connections)\n        try:\n            with mock.patch.object(\n                cache._class, \"disconnect_all\", autospec=True\n            ) as mock_disconnect:\n                signals.request_finished.send(self.__class__)\n                self.assertIs(mock_disconnect.called, self.should_disconnect_on_close)\n        finally:\n            signals.request_finished.connect(close_old_connections)\n\n    def test_set_many_returns_failing_keys(self):\n        def fail_set_multi(mapping, *args, **kwargs):\n            return                return self._time\n\n            def sleep(self, seconds):\n                self._time += seconds\n\n        mocked_time = ManualTickingTime()\n        with (\n },\n)\nclass CacheMiddlewareTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    factory = RequestFactory()\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        self.default_cache = caches[\"default\"]\n        self.addCleanup(self.default_cache.clear)\n        self.other_cache = caches[\"other\"]\n        self.addCleanup(self.other_cache.clear)\n\n    def test_constructor(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The constructor is correctly distinguishing between usage of\n        CacheMiddleware as Middleware vs. usage of CacheMiddleware as view\n        decorator and setting attributes appropriately.\n        \"\"\"\n        # If only one argument is passed in construction, it's being used as\n        # middleware.\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n\n        # Now test object attributes against values defined in setUp above\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_timeout, 30)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.key_prefix, \"middlewareprefix\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n        # If more arguments are being passed in construction, it's being used\n        # as a decorator. First, test with \"defaults\":\n        as_view_decorator = CacheMiddleware(\n            empty_response, cache_alias=None, key_prefix=None\n        )\n\n        self.assertEqual(\n            as_view_decorator.cache_timeout, 30\n        )  # Timeout value for 'default' cache, i.e. 30\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.key_prefix, \"\")\n        # Value of DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS from django.core.cache\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.cache_alias, \"default\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.cache, self.default_cache)\n\n        # Next, test with custom values:\n        as_view_decorator_with_custom = CacheMiddleware(\n            hello_world_view, cache_timeout=60, cache_alias=\"other\", key_prefix=\"foo\"\n        )\n\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache_timeout, 60)\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.key_prefix, \"foo\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n    def self.assertIsNone(middleware.page_timeout)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.key_prefix, \"middlewareprefix\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n    def test_fetch_cache_middleware_constructor(self):\n        middleware = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n test_middleware(self):\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view)\n        prefix_middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view, key_prefix=\"prefix1\")\n        timeout_middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view, cache_timeout=1)\n\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # Put the request through the request middleware\n        result = middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNone(result)\n\n        response = hello_world_view(request, \"1\")\n\n        # Now put the response through the response middleware\n        response = middleware.process_response(request, response)\n\n        # Repeating the request should result in a cache hit\n        result = middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(result)\n        self.assertEqual(result.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # The same request through a different middleware won't hit\n        result = prefix_middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNone(result)\n\n        # The same request with a timeout _will_ hit\n        result = timeout_middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(result)\n        self.assertEqual(result.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n    def test_view_decorator(self):\n        # decorate the same view with different cache decorators\n        default_view = cache_page(3)(hello_world_view)\n        default_with_prefix_view = cache_page(3, key_prefix=\"prefix1\")(hello_world_view)\n\n        explicit_default_view = cache_page(3, cache=\"default\")(hello_world_view)\n        explicit_default_with_prefix_view = cache_page(\n            3, cache=\"default\", key_prefix=\"prefix1\"\n        )(hello_world_view)\n\n        other_view = cache_page(1, cache=\"other\")(hello_world_view)\n        other_with_prefix_view = cache_page(1, cache=\"other\", key_prefix=\"prefix2\")(\n            hello_world_view\n        )\n\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # Request the view once\n        response = default_view(request, \"1\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Request again -- hit the cache\n        response = default_view(request, \"2\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Requesting the same view with the explicit cache should yield the\n        # same result\n        response = explicit_default_view(request, \"3\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Requesting with a prefix will hit a different cache key\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"4\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # Hitting the same view again gives a cache hit\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"5\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # And going back to the implicit cache will hit the same cache\n        response = default_with_prefix_view(request, \"6\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # Requesting from an alternate cache won't hit cache\n        response = other_view(request, \"7\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 7\")\n\n        # But a repeated hit will hit cache\n        response = other_view(request, \"8\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 7\")\n\n        # And prefixing the alternate cache yields yet another cache entry\n        response = other_with_prefix_view(request, \"9\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 9\")\n\n        # But if we wait a couple of seconds...\n        time.sleep(2)\n\n        # ... the default cache will still hit\n        caches[\"default\"]\n        response = default_view(request, \"11\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # ... the default cache with a prefix will still hit\n        response = default_with_prefix_view(request, \"12\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # ... the explicit default cache will still hit\n        response = explicit_default_view(request, \"13\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # ... the explicit default cache with a prefix will still hit\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"14\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # .. but a rapidly expiring cache won't hit\n        response = other_view(request, \"15\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 15\")\n\n        # .. even if it has a prefix\n        response = other_with_prefix_view(request, \"16\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 16\")\n\n    @retry()\n    def test_cache_page_timeout(self):\n        # Page timeout takes precedence over the \"max-age\" section of the\n        # \"Cache-Control\".\n        tests = [\n            (1, 3),  # max_age < page_timeout.\n            (3, 1),  # max_age > page_timeout.\n        ]\n        for max_age, page_timeout in tests:\n            with self.subTest(max_age=max_age, page_timeout=page_timeout):\n                view = cache_page(timeout=page_timeout)(\n                    cache_control(max_age=max_age)(hello_world_view)\n                )\n                request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n                response = view(request, \"1\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n                time.sleep(1)\n                response = view(request, \"2\")\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    response.content,\n                    b\"Hello World 1\" if page_timeout > max_age else b\"Hello World 2\",\n                )\n            cache.clear()\n\n    def test_cache_control_not_cached(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Responses with 'Cache-Control: private/no-cache/no-store' are\n        not cached.\n        \"\"\"\n        for cc in (\"private\", \"no-cache\", \"no-store\"):\n            with self.subTest(cache_control=cc):\n                view_with_cache = cache_page(3)(\n                    cache_control(**{cc: True})(hello_world_view)\n                )\n                request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n                response = view_with_cache(request, \"1\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n                response = view_with_cache(request, \"2\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 2\")\n\n    def test_sensitive_cookie_not_cached(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Django must prevent caching of responses that set a user-specific (and\n        maybe security sensitive) cookie in response to a cookie-less request.\n        \"\"\"\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n        csrf_middleware = CsrfViewMiddleware(csrf_view)\n        csrf_middleware.process_view(request, csrf_view, (), {})\n        cache_middleware = CacheMiddleware(csrf_middleware)\n\n        self.assertIsNone(cache_middleware.process_request(request))\n        cache_middleware(request)\n\n        # Inserting a CSRF cookie in a cookie-less request prevented caching.\n        self.assertIsNone(cache_middleware.process_request(request))\n\n    def test_304_response_has_http_caching_headers_but_not_cached(self):\n        original_view = mock.Mock(return_value=HttpResponseNotModified())\n        view = cache_page(2)(original_view)\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n        # The view shouldn't be cached on the second call.\n        view(request).close()\n        response = view(request)\n        response.close()\n        self.assertEqual(original_view.call_count, 2)\n        self.assertIsInstance(response, HttpResponseNotModified)\n        self.assertIn(\"Cache-Control\", response)\n        self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n\n    def test_per_thread(self):\n        \"\"\"The cache instance is different for each thread.\"\"\"\n        thread_caches = []\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n\n        def runner():\n            thread_caches.append(middleware.cache)\n\n        for _ in range(2):\n            thread = threading.Thread(target=runner)\n            thread.start()\n            thread.join()\n\n        self.assertIsNot(thread_caches[0], thread_caches[1])\n\n    def test_cache_control_max_age(self):\n        view = cache_page(2)(hello_world_view)\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # First request. Freshly created response gets returned with no Age\n        # header.\n        with mock.patch.object(\n            time, \"time\", return_value=1468749600\n        ):  # Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:00 GMT\n            response = view(request, 1)\n            response.close()\n            self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Expires\"], \"Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:02 GMT\")\n            self.assertIn(\"Cache-Control\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Cache-Control\"], \"max-age=2\")\n            self.assertNotIn(\"Age\", response)\n\n        # Second request one second later. 2016 10:00:01 GMT\n            response = view(request, 1)\n            response.close()\n            self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Expires\"], \"Sun, self.assertEqual(self.session.pop(\"cat\"), \"dog\")\n\n    async def test_store_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"cat\", \"dog\")\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n        self.assertEqual(await self.session.apop(\"cat\"), \"dog\")\n\n    def test_pop(self):\n        self.session[\"some key\"] = \"exists\"\n        # Need to reset these to pretend we haven't accessed it:\n        self.accessed = False\n        self.modified = False\n\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.pop(\"some key\"), \"exists\")\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n        self.assertIsNone(self.session.get(\"some key\"))\n\n    async def test_pop_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"some key\", \"exists\")\n        # Need to reset these to pretend we haven't accessed it:\n        self.accessed = False\n        self.modified = False\n\n        self.assertEqual(await self.session.apop(\"some key\"), \"exists\")\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n        self.assertIsNone(await self.session.aget(\"some key\"))\n\n    def       self.assertIs(self.session.modified, False)\n\n    async def test_pop_default_async(self):\n        self.assertEqual(\n            await self.session.apop(\"some key\", \"does not exist\"), \"does not exist\"\n        )\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, False)\n\n    def test_pop_default_named_argument(self):\n self.assertIs(self.session.modified, False)\n\n    async def test_pop_default_named_argument_async(self):\n        self.assertEqual(\n            await self.session.apop(\"some key\", default=\"does     with self.assertRaises(KeyError):\n            await self.session.apop(\"some key\")\n\n    def test_setdefault(self):\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.setdefault(\"foo\", \"bar\"), self.session.accessed = False\n        self.assertEqual(list(self.session.values()), [1])\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, False)\n\n       self.assertEqual(list(self.session.keys()), [\"x\"])\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, False)\n\n    async def        self.session.modified = False\n        self.session.accessed = False\n        self.assertEqual(list(self.session.items()), [(\"x\", 1)])\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n\n    def test_save(self):\n        self.session.save()\n        self.assertIs(self.session.exists(self.session.session_key), True)\n\n    async def self.session.save()\n        self.session.delete(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.exists(self.session.session_key), False)\n\n    async def test_delete_async(self):\n        await self.session.asave()\n        await self.session.adelete(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(await self.session.aexists(self.session.session_key), False)\n\n    def test_flush(self):\n        self.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        self.session.save()\n        prev_key = self.session.session_key\n        self.session.flush()\n        self.assertIs(self.session.exists(prev_key), False)\n        self.assertNotEqual(self.session.session_key, prev_key)\n        self.assertIsNone(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n\n    async def test_flush_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"foo\", \"bar\")\n        await self.session.asave()\n        prev_key = self.session.session_key\n        await self.session.aflush()\n        self.assertIs(await self.session.aexists(prev_key), False)\n        self.assertNotEqual(self.session.session_key, prev_key)\n        self.assertIsNone(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.modified, True)\n        self.assertIs(self.session.accessed, True)\n\n    def test_cycle(self):\n        self.session[\"a\"], self.session[\"b\"] = \"c\", \"d\"\n        self.session.save()\n        prev_key = self.session.session_key\n        prev_data = list(self.session.items())\n        self.session.cycle_key()\n        self.assertIs(self.session.exists(prev_key), False)\n        self.assertNotEqual(self.session.session_key, prev_key)\n        self.assertEqual(list(self.session.items()), prev_data)\n\n    async def test_cycle_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"a\", \"c\")\n        await self.session.aset(\"b\", \"d\")\n        await self.session.asave()\n        prev_key = self.session.session_key\n        prev_data = list(await self.session.aitems())\n        await self.session.acycle_key()\n        self.assertIs(await self.session.aexists(prev_key), False)\n        self.assertNotEqual(self.session.session_key, prev_key)\n        self.assertEqual(list(await self.session.aitems()), prev_data)\n\n    def test_cycle_with_no_session_cache(self):\n        self.session[\"a\"], self.session[\"b\"] = \"c\", \"d\"\n        self.session.save()\n        prev_data = self.session.items()\n        self.session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(hasattr(self.session, \"_session_cache\"), False)\n        self.session.cycle_key()\n        self.assertCountEqual(self.session.items(), prev_data)\n\n    async def test_cycle_with_no_session_cache_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"a\", \"c\")\n        await self.session.aset(\"b\", \"d\")\n        await self.session.asave()\n        prev_data = await self.session.aitems()\n        self.session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(hasattr(self.session, \"_session_cache\"), False)\n        await self.session.acycle_key()\n        self.assertCountEqual(await self.session.aitems(), prev_data)\n\n    def test_save_doesnt_clear_data(self):\n        self.session[\"a\"] = \"b\"\n        self.session.save()\n        self.assertEqual(self.session[\"a\"], \"b\")\n\n    async def test_save_doesnt_clear_data_async(self):\n        await self.session.aset(\"a\", \"b\")\n        await self.session.asave()\n        self.assertEqual(await self.session.aget(\"a\"), \"b\")\n\n    def test_invalid_key(self):\n        # Submitting an invalid session key (either by guessing, or if the db\n        # has removed the key) results in a new key being generated.\n        try:\n            session = self.backend(\"1\")\n            session.save()\n            self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"1\")\n            self.assertIsNone(session.get(\"cat\"))\n            session.delete()\n        finally:\n            # Some backends leave a stale cache entry for the invalid\n            # session key; make sure that entry is manually deleted\n            session.delete(\"1\")\n\n    async def test_invalid_key_async(self):\n        # Submitting an invalid session key (either by guessing, or if the db\n        # has removed the key) results in a new key being generated.\n        try:\n            session = self.backend(\"1\")\n            await session.asave()\n            self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"1\")\n            self.assertIsNone(await session.aget(\"cat\"))\n            await session.adelete()\n        finally:\n            # Some backends leave a stale cache entry for the invalid\n            # session key; make sure that entry is manually deleted\n            await session.adelete(\"1\")\n\n    def test_session_key_empty_string_invalid(self):\n        \"\"\"Falsey values (Such as an empty string) are rejected.\"\"\"\n        self.session._session_key = \"\"\n        self.assertIsNone(self.session.session_key)\n\n    def test_session_key_too_short_invalid(self):\n        \"\"\"Strings shorter than 8 characters are rejected.\"\"\"\n        self.session._session_key   self.session._session_key = \"12345678\"\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.session_key, \"12345678\")\n\n    def test_session_key_is_read_only(self):\n        def set_session_key(session):\n            session.session_key = session._get_new_session_key()\n\n        with self.assertRaises(AttributeError):\n            set_session_key(self.session)\n\n    # Custom session expiry\n    def test_default_expiry(self):\n        # A normal session has a max age equal to settings\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.get_expiry_age(), settings.SESSION_COOKIE_AGE)\n\n        # So does a custom session with an idle expiration time of 0 (but it'll\n        # expire at browser close)\n        self.session.set_expiry(0)\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.get_expiry_age(), settings.SESSION_COOKIE_AGE)\n\n    async def test_default_expiry_async(self):\n        # A normal session has a max age equal to settings.\n        self.assertEqual(\n            await self.session.aget_expiry_age(), settings.SESSION_COOKIE_AGE\n        )\n        # So does a custom session with an idle expiration time of 0 (but it'll\n        # expire at browser close).\n        await self.session.aset_expiry(0)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            await self.session.aget_expiry_age(), settings.SESSION_COOKIE_AGE\n        )\n\n    def test_custom_expiry_seconds(self):\n        modification = timezone.now()\n\n        self.session.set_expiry(10)\n\n        date = self.session.get_expiry_date(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(date, modification + timedelta(seconds=10))\n\n        age = self.session.get_expiry_age(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(age, 10)\n\n    async def test_custom_expiry_seconds_async(self):\n        modification = timezone.now()\n\n        await self.session.aset_expiry(10)\n\n        date = await self.session.aget_expiry_date(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(date, modification + timedelta(seconds=10))\n\n        age = await self.session.aget_expiry_age(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(age, 10)\n\n    def test_custom_expiry_timedelta(self):\n        modification = timezone.now()\n\n        # Mock timezone.now, because set_expiry calls it on this code path.\n        original_now = timezone.now\n        try:\n            timezone.now = lambda: modification\n            self.session.set_expiry(timedelta(seconds=10))\n        finally:\n            timezone.now = original_now\n\n        date = self.session.get_expiry_date(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(date, modification + timedelta(seconds=10))\n\n        age = self.session.get_expiry_age(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(age, 10)\n\n    async def test_custom_expiry_timedelta_async(self):\n        modification = timezone.now()\n\n        # Mock timezone.now, because set_expiry calls it on this         timezone.now = original_now\n\n        date = await self.session.aget_expiry_date(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(date, modification + timedelta(seconds=10))\n\n        age = await self.session.aget_expiry_age(modification=modification)\n        self.assertEqual(age, 10)\n\n    def test_custom_expiry_datetime(self):\n        modification = timezone.now()\n\n def test_get_expire_at_browser_close(self):\n        # Tests get_expire_at_browser_close with different settings and\n        # different set_expiry calls\n        with override_settings(SESSION_EXPIRE_AT_BROWSER_CLOSE=False):\n            self.session.set_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            self.session.set_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            self.session.set_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n        with override_settings(SESSION_EXPIRE_AT_BROWSER_CLOSE=True):\n            self.session.set_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            self.session.set_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            self.session.set_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(self.session.get_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n    async def test_get_expire_at_browser_close_async(self):\n        # Tests self.session.aset_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n        with override_settings(SESSION_EXPIRE_AT_BROWSER_CLOSE=True):\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n    def test_decode(self):\n        # Ensure we can decode what we encode\n        data = {\"a test key\": \"a test value\"}\n        encoded = self.session.encode(data)\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), data)\n\n    def test_decode_failure_logged_to_security(self):\n        tests = [\n            base64.b64encode(b\"flaskdj:alkdjf\").decode(\"ascii\"),\n            \"bad:encoded:value\",\n        ]\n        for encoded in tests:\n            with self.subTest(encoded=encoded):\n                with self.assertLogs(\n                    \"django.security.SuspiciousSession\", \"WARNING\"\n                ) as cm:\n                    self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), {})\n                # The failed decode is logged.\n                self.assertIn(\"Session data corrupted\", cm.output[0])\n\n    def test_decode_serializer_exception(self):\n        signer = TimestampSigner(salt=self.session.key_salt)\n        encoded = signer.sign(b\"invalid data\")\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), {})\n\n    def test_actual_expiry(self):\n        old_session_key = None\n        new_session_key = None\n        try:\n            self.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n            self.session.set_expiry(-timedelta(seconds=10))\n            self.session.save()\n            old_session_key = self.session.session_key\n            # With an expiry date in the past, the session expires instantly.\n            new_session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n            new_session_key = new_session.session_key\n            self.assertNotIn(\"foo\", new_session)\n        finally:\n            self.session.delete(old_session_key)\n            self.session.delete(new_session_key)\n\n    async def test_actual_expiry_async(self):\n        old_session_key = None\n        new_session_key = None\n        try:\n            await self.session.aset(\"foo\", \"bar\")\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(-timedelta(seconds=10))\n            await self.session.asave()\n            old_session_key = self.session.session_key\n            # With an expiry date in the past, the session expires instantly.\n            new_session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n            new_session_key = new_session.session_key\n            self.assertIs(await new_session.ahas_key(\"foo\"), False)\n        finally:\n            await self.session.adelete(old_session_key)\n            await self.session.adelete(new_session_key)\n\n    def test_session_load_does_not_create_record(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Loading an unknown session key does not create a session record.\n\n        Creating session records on load is a DOS vulnerability.\n        \"\"\"\n        session = self.backend(\"someunknownkey\")\n        session.load()\n\n        self.assertIsNone(session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(session.exists(session.session_key), False)\n        # provided unknown key was cycled, not reused\n        self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"someunknownkey\")\n\n    async def test_session_load_does_not_create_record_async(self):\n        session = self.backend(\"someunknownkey\")\n        await session.aload()\n\n        self.assertIsNone(session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(await session.aexists(session.session_key), False)\n        # Provided unknown key was cycled, not reused.\n        self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"someunknownkey\")\n\n    def test_session_save_does_not_resurrect_session_logged_out_in_other_context(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Sessions shouldn't be resurrected by a concurrent request.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Create new session.\n        s1 = self.backend()\n        s1[\"test_data\"] = \"value1\"\n        s1.save(must_create=True)\n\n        # Logout in another context.\n        s2 = self.backend(s1.session_key)\n        s2.delete()\n\n        # Modify session in first context.\n        s1[\"test_data\"] = \"value2\"\n        with self.assertRaises(UpdateError):\n            # This should throw an exception as the session is deleted, not\n            # resurrect the session.\n            s1.save()\n\n        self.assertEqual(s1.load(), {})\n\n    async def test_session_asave_does_not_resurrect_session_logged_out_in_other_context(\n        self,\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Sessions shouldn't be resurrected by a concurrent request.\"\"\"\n        # Create new session.\n        s1 = self.backend()\n        await s1.aset(\"test_data\", \"value1\")\n        await s1.asave(must_create=True)\n\n        # Logout in another context.\n        s2 = self.backend(s1.session_key)\n        await s2.adelete()\n\n        # Modify session in first context.\n        await s1.aset(\"test_data\", \"value2\")\n        with self.assertRaises(UpdateError):\n            # This should throw an exception as the session is deleted, not\n            # resurrect the session.\n            await s1.asave()\n\n        self.assertEqual(await s1.aload(), {})\n\n\nclass DatabaseSessionTests(SessionTestsMixin, TestCase):\n    backend = DatabaseSession\n    session_engine = \"django.contrib.sessions.backends.db\"\n\n    @property\n    def model(self):\n        return self.backend.get_model_class()\n\n    def test_session_str(self):\n        \"Session repr should be the session key.\"\n        self.session[\"x\"] = 1\n        self.session.save()\n\n        session_key = self.session.session_key\n        s = self.model.objects.get(session_key=session_key)\n\n        self.assertEqual(str(s), session_key)\n\n    def test_session_get_decoded(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test we can use Session.get_decoded to retrieve data stored\n        in normal way\n        \"\"\"\n        self.session[\"x\"] = 1\n FileSession\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        # Do file session tests in an isolated directory, and kill it after\n        # we're done.\n        self.original_session_file_path = settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH\n        self.temp_session_store = settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH = self.mkdtemp()\n        self.addCleanup(shutil.rmtree, self.temp_session_store)\n        # Reset the file session backend's internal caches\n        if hasattr(self.backend, \"_storage_path\"):\n            del self.backend._storage_path\n        super().setUp()\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        super().tearDown()\n        settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH = self.original_session_file_path\n\n    def mkdtemp(self):\n        return tempfile.mkdtemp()\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_FILE_PATH=\"/if/this/directory/exists/you/have/a/weird/computer\",\n    )\n    def test_configuration_check(self):\n        del self.backend._storage_path\n        # Make sure the file backend checks for a good storage dir\n        with self.assertRaises(ImproperlyConfigured):\n            self.backend()\n\n    def test_invalid_key_backslash(self):\n        # Ensure we don't allow directory-traversal.\n        # This is tested directly on _key_to_file, as load() will swallow\n        # a SuspiciousOperation in the same way as an OSError - by creating\n        # a new session, making it unclear whether the slashes were detected.\n        with self.assertRaises(InvalidSessionKey):\n            self.backend()._key_to_file(\"a\\\\b\\\\c\")\n\n    def test_invalid_key_forwardslash(self):\n        # Ensure we don't allow directory-traversal\n        with self.assertRaises(InvalidSessionKey):\n            self.backend()._key_to_file(\"a/b/c\")\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_ENGINE=\"django.contrib.sessions.backends.file\",\n        SESSION_COOKIE_AGE=0,\n    )\n    def test_clearsessions_command(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test clearsessions command for clearing expired sessions.\n        \"\"\"\n        storage_path = self.backend._get_storage_path()\n        file_prefix = settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME\n\n        def count_sessions():\n            return len(\n                [\n                    session_file\n                    for session_file in os.listdir(storage_path)\n                    if session_file.startswith(file_prefix)\n                ]\n            )\n\n        self.assertEqual(0, count_sessions())\n\n        # One object in the future\n        self.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        self.session.set_expiry(3600)\n        self.session.save()\n\n        # One object in the past\n        other_session = self.backend()\n        other_session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        other_session.set_expiry(-3600)\n        other_session.save()\n\n        # One object in the present without an expiry (should be deleted since\n        # its modification time + SESSION_COOKIE_AGE will be in the past when\n        # clearsessions runs).\n        other_session2 = self.backend()\n        other_session2[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        other_session2.save()\n\n        # Three sessions are in the filesystem before clearsessions...\n        self.assertEqual(3, count_sessions())\n        management.call_command(\"clearsessions\")\n        # ... and two are deleted.\n        self.assertEqual(1, count_sessions())\n\n\nclass FileSessionPathLibTests(FileSessionTests):\n    def mkdtemp(self):\n        tmp_dir = super().mkdtemp()\n        return Path(tmp_dir)\n\n\nclass get_response_touching_session(request):\n        request.session[\"hello\"] = \"world\"\n        return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True)\n    def test_secure_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertIs(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"secure\"], True)\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True)\n    def test_httponly_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertIs(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"httponly\"], True)\n        self.assertIn(\n            cookies.Morsel._reserved[\"httponly\"],\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE=\"Strict\")\n    def test_samesite_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"samesite\"], \"Strict\"\n        )\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=False)\n    def test_no_httponly_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n        response = middleware(request)\n str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    def test_session_save_on_500(self):\n        def response_500(request):\n            response = HttpResponse(\"Horrible error\")\n            response.status_code = 500\n            request.session[\"hello\"] = \"world\"\n            return response\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        SessionMiddleware(response_500)(request)\n\n        # The value wasn't saved above.\n        self.assertNotIn(\"hello\", request.session.load())\n\n    def test_session_save_on_5xx(self):\n        def response\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        SessionMiddleware(response_503)(request)\n\n        # The value wasn't saved above.\n        self.assertNotIn(\"hello\", request.session.load())\n\n    def test_session_update_error_redirect(self):\n        def response_delete_session(request):\n            request.session = DatabaseSession()\n            request.session.save(must_create=True)\n            request.session.delete()\n            return HttpResponse()\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/foo/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_delete_session)\n\n        msg = (\n            \"The request's session was deleted before the request completed. \"\n            \"The user may have logged out in a concurrent request, for example.\"\n        )\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(SessionInterrupted, msg):\n            # Handle the response through the middleware. It will try to save\n            # the deleted session which will cause an UpdateError that's caught\n            # and raised as a SessionInterrupted.\n            middleware(request)\n\n    def test_session_delete_on_end(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Before deleting, there has to be an existing cookie\n        request.COOKIES[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME] = \"abc\"\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # The cookie was deleted, not recreated.\n        # A deleted cookie header looks like:\n        #  \"Set-Cookie: sessionid=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; \"\n        #  \"Max-Age=0; Path=/\"\n        self.assertEqual(\n            'Set-Cookie: {}=\"\"; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; '\n            \"Max-Age=0; Path=/; SameSite={}\".format(\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,\n            ),\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n        # SessionMiddleware sets 'Vary: Cookie' to prevent the 'Set-Cookie'\n        # from being cached.\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN=\".example.local\", SESSION_COOKIE_PATH=\"/example/\"\n    )\n    def test_session_delete_on_end_with_custom_domain_and_path(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Before deleting, there has to be an existing cookie\n        request.COOKIES[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME] = \"abc\"\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # The cookie was deleted, not recreated.\n        # A deleted cookie header with a custom domain and path looks like:\n        #  Set-Cookie: sessionid=; Domain=.example.local;\n        #              expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; Max-Age=0;\n        #              Path=/example/\n        self.assertEqual(\n            'Set-Cookie: {}=\"\"; Domain=.example.local; expires=Thu, '\n            \"01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; Max-Age=0; Path=/example/; SameSite={}\".format(\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,\n            ),\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    def test_flush_empty_without_session_cookie_doesnt_set_cookie(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # A cookie should not be set.\n        self.assertEqual(response.cookies, {})\n        # The session is accessed so \"Vary: Cookie\" should be set.\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n    def test_empty_session_saved(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        If a session is emptied of data but still has a key, it should still\n        be updated.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def response_set_session(request):\n            # Set a session key and some data.\n            request.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_set_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware.\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertEqual(tuple(request.session.items()), ((\"foo\", \"bar\"),))\n        # A cookie should be set, along with Vary: Cookie.\n        self.assertIn(\n            \"Set-Cookie: sessionid=%s\" % request.session.session_key,\n            str(response.cookies),\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n        # Empty the session data.\n        del request.session[\"foo\"]\n        # Handle the response through the middleware.\n        response = HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n        response = middleware.process_response(request, response)\n        self.assertEqual(dict(request.session.values()), {})\n        session = Session.objects.get(session_key=request.session.session_key)\n        self.assertEqual(session.get_decoded(), {})\n        # While the session is empty, it hasn't been flushed so a cookie should\n        # still be set, along with Vary: Cookie.\n        self.assertGreater(len(request.session.session_key), 8)\n        self.assertIn(\n            \"Set-Cookie: sessionid=%s\" % request.session.session_key,\n            str(response.cookies),\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n\nclass CookieSessionTests(SessionTestsMixin, SimpleTestCase):\n    backend = CookieSession\n\n    def test_save(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        This test tested exists() in the other session backends, but that\n        doesn't make sense for us.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    async def test_save_async(self):\n        pass\n\n    def test_cycle(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        This test tested cycle_key() which would create a new session\n        key for the same session data. self.session.aset_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n        with override_settings(SESSION_EXPIRE_AT_BROWSER_CLOSE=True):\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(10)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), False)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(0)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(None)\n            self.assertIs(await self.session.aget_expire_at_browser_close(), True)\n\n    def test_decode(self):\n        # Ensure we can decode what we encode\n        data = {\"a test key\": \"a test value\"}\n        encoded = self.session.encode(data)\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), data)\n\n    def test_decode_failure_logged_to_security(self):\n        tests = [\n            base64.b64encode(b\"flaskdj:alkdjf\").decode(\"ascii\"),\n            \"bad:encoded:value\",\n        ]\n        for encoded in tests:\n            with self.subTest(encoded=encoded):\n                with self.assertLogs(\n                    \"django.security.SuspiciousSession\", \"WARNING\"\n                ) as cm:\n                    self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), {})\n                # The failed decode is logged.\n                self.assertIn(\"Session data corrupted\", cm.output[0])\n\n    def test_decode_serializer_exception(self):\n        signer = TimestampSigner(salt=self.session.key_salt)\n        encoded = signer.sign(b\"invalid data\")\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.decode(encoded), {})\n\n    def test_actual_expiry(self):\n        old_session_key = None\n        new_session_key = None\n        try:\n            self.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n            self.session.set_expiry(-timedelta(seconds=10))\n            self.session.save()\n            old_session_key = self.session.session_key\n            # With an expiry date in the past, the session expires instantly.\n            new_session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n            new_session_key = new_session.session_key\n            self.assertNotIn(\"foo\", new_session)\n        finally:\n            self.session.delete(old_session_key)\n            self.session.delete(new_session_key)\n\n    async def test_actual_expiry_async(self):\n        old_session_key = None\n        new_session_key = None\n        try:\n            await self.session.aset(\"foo\", \"bar\")\n            await self.session.aset_expiry(-timedelta(seconds=10))\n            await self.session.asave()\n            old_session_key = self.session.session_key\n            # With an expiry date in the past, the session expires instantly.\n            new_session = self.backend(self.session.session_key)\n            new_session_key = new_session.session_key\n            self.assertIs(await new_session.ahas_key(\"foo\"), False)\n        finally:\n            await self.session.adelete(old_session_key)\n            await self.session.adelete(new_session_key)\n\n    def test_session_load_does_not_create_record(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Loading an unknown session key does not create a session record.\n\n        Creating session records on load is a DOS vulnerability.\n        \"\"\"\n        session = self.backend(\"someunknownkey\")\n        session.load()\n\n        self.assertIsNone(session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(session.exists(session.session_key), False)\n        # provided unknown key was cycled, not reused\n        self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"someunknownkey\")\n\n    async def test_session_load_does_not_create_record_async(self):\n        session = self.backend(\"someunknownkey\")\n        await session.aload()\n\n        self.assertIsNone(session.session_key)\n        self.assertIs(await session.aexists(session.session_key), False)\n        # Provided unknown key was cycled, not reused.\n        self.assertNotEqual(session.session_key, \"someunknownkey\")\n\n    def test_session_save_does_not_resurrect_session_logged_out_in_other_context(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Sessions shouldn't be resurrected by a concurrent request.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Create new session.\n        s1 = self.backend()\n        s1[\"test_data\"] = \"value1\"\n        s1.save(must_create=True)\n\n        # Logout in another context.\n        s2 = self.backend(s1.session_key)\n        s2.delete()\n\n        # Modify session in first context.\n        s1[\"test_data\"] = \"value2\"\n        with self.assertRaises(UpdateError):\n            # This should throw an exception as the session is deleted, not\n            # resurrect the session.\n            s1.save()\n\n        self.assertEqual(s1.load(), {})\n\n    async def test_session_asave_does_not_resurrect_session_logged_out_in_other_context(\n        self,\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Sessions shouldn't be resurrected by a concurrent request.\"\"\"\n        # Create new session.\n        s1 = self.backend()\n        await s1.aset(\"test_data\", \"value1\")\n        await s1.asave(must_create=True)\n\n        # Logout in another context.\n        s2 = self.backend(s1.session_key)\n        await s2.adelete()\n\n        # Modify session in first context.\n        await s1.aset(\"test_data\", \"value2\")\n        with self.assertRaises(UpdateError):\n            # This should throw an exception as the session is deleted, not\n            # resurrect the session.\n            await s1.asave()\n\n        self.assertEqual(await s1.aload(), {})\n\n\nclass DatabaseSessionTests(SessionTestsMixin, TestCase):\n    backend = DatabaseSession\n    session_engine = \"django.contrib.sessions.backends.db\"\n\n      \"\"\"\n        Test SessionManager.save method\n        \"\"\"\n        # Create a session\n        self.session[\"y\"] = 1\n        self.session.save()\n\n        s = other_session.set_expiry(-3600)\n        other_session.save()\n\n        # Two sessions are in the database before clearsessions...\n        self.assertEqual(2, self.model.objects.count())\n self.model.objects.count())\n\n    async def test_aclear_expired(self):\n        self.assertEqual(await self.model.objects.acount(), 0)\n\n        # Object in the future.\n        await FileSession\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        # Do file session tests in an isolated directory, and kill it after\n        # we're done.\n        self.original_session_file_path = settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH\n        self.temp_session_store = settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH = self.mkdtemp()\n        self.addCleanup(shutil.rmtree, self.temp_session_store)\n        # Reset the file session backend's internal caches\n        if hasattr(self.backend, \"_storage_path\"):\n            del self.backend._storage_path\n        super().setUp()\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        super().tearDown()\n        settings.SESSION_FILE_PATH = self.original_session_file_path\n\n    def mkdtemp(self):\n        return tempfile.mkdtemp()\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_FILE_PATH=\"/if/this/directory/exists/you/have/a/weird/computer\",\n    )\n    def test_configuration_check(self):\n        del self.backend._storage_path\n        # Make sure the file backend checks for a good storage dir\n        with self.assertRaises(ImproperlyConfigured):\n            self.backend()\n\n    def test_invalid_key_backslash(self):\n        # Ensure we don't allow directory-traversal.\n        # This is tested directly on _key_to_file, as load() will swallow\n        # a SuspiciousOperation in the same way as an OSError - by creating\n        # a new session, making it unclear whether the slashes were detected.\n        with self.assertRaises(InvalidSessionKey):\n            self.backend()._key_to_file(\"a\\\\b\\\\c\")\n\n    def test_invalid_key_forwardslash(self):\n        # Ensure we don't allow directory-traversal\n        with self.assertRaises(InvalidSessionKey):\n            self.backend()._key_to_file(\"a/b/c\")\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_ENGINE=\"django.contrib.sessions.backends.file\",\n        SESSION_COOKIE_AGE=0,\n    )\n    def test_clearsessions_command(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test clearsessions command for clearing expired sessions.\n        \"\"\"\n        storage_path = self.backend._get_storage_path()\n        file_prefix = settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME\n\n        def count_sessions():\n            return len(\n                [\n                    session_file\n                    for session_file in os.listdir(storage_path)\n                    if session_file.startswith(file_prefix)\n                ]\n            )\n\n        self.assertEqual(0, count_sessions())\n\n        # One object in the future\n        self.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        self.session.set_expiry(3600)\n        self.session.save()\n\n        # One object in the past\n        other_session = self.backend()\n        other_session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        other_session.set_expiry(-3600)\n        other_session.save()\n\n        # One object in the present without an expiry (should be deleted since\n        # its modification time + SESSION_COOKIE_AGE will be in the past when\n        # clearsessions runs).\n        other_session2 = self.backend()\n        other_session2[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n        other_session2.save()\n\n        # Three sessions are in the filesystem before clearsessions...\n        self.assertEqual(3, count_sessions())\n        management.call_command(\"clearsessions\")\n        # ... and two are deleted.\n        self.assertEqual(1, count_sessions())\n\n\nclass FileSessionPathLibTests(FileSessionTests):\n    def mkdtemp(self):\n        tmp_dir = super().mkdtemp()\n        return Path(tmp_dir)\n\n\nclass CacheSessionTests(SessionTestsMixin, SimpleTestCase):\n    backend = CacheSession\n\n    # Some backends might issue a warning\n    @ignore_warnings(module=\"django.core.cache.backends.base\")\n    def test_load_overlong_key(self):\n        self.session._session_key = (string.ascii_letters + string.digits) * 20\n        self.assertEqual(self.session.load(), {})\n\n    def test_default_cache(self):\n        self.session.save()\n        self.assertIsNotNone(caches[\"default\"].get(self.session.cache_key))\n\n    @override_settings(\n        CACHES={\n test_create_and_save_async(self):\n        self.session = self.backend()\n        await self.session.acreate()\n        await self.session.asave()\n        self.assertIsNotNone(caches[\"default\"].get(await self.session.acache_key()))\n\n\nclass SessionMiddlewareTests(TestCase):\n    request_factory = RequestFactory()\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_response_touching_session(request):\n        request.session[\"hello\"] = \"world\"\n        return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True)\n    def test_secure_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertIs(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"secure\"], True)\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True)\n    def test_httponly_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertIs(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"httponly\"], True)\n        self.assertIn(\n            cookies.Morsel._reserved[\"httponly\"],\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE=\"Strict\")\n    def test_samesite_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"samesite\"], \"Strict\"\n        )\n\n    @override_settings(SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=False)\n    def test_no_httponly_session_cookie(self):\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(self.get_response_touching_session)\n        response = middleware(request)\n        self.assertEqual(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME][\"httponly\"], \"\")\n        self.assertNotIn(\n            cookies.Morsel._reserved[\"httponly\"],\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    def test_session_save_on_500(self):\n        def response_500(request):\n            response = HttpResponse(\"Horrible error\")\n            response.status_code = 500\n            request.session[\"hello\"] = \"world\"\n            return response\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        SessionMiddleware(response_500)(request)\n\n        # The value wasn't saved above.\n        self.assertNotIn(\"hello\", request.session.load())\n\n    def test_session_save_on_5xx(self):\n        def response_503(request):\n            response = HttpResponse(\"Service Unavailable\")\n            response.status_code = 503\n            request.session[\"hello\"] = \"world\"\n            return response\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        SessionMiddleware(response_503)(request)\n\n        # The value wasn't saved above.\n        self.assertNotIn(\"hello\", request.session.load())\n\n    def test_session_update_error_redirect(self):\n        def response_delete_session(request):\n            request.session = DatabaseSession()\n            request.session.save(must_create=True)\n            request.session.delete()\n            return HttpResponse()\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/foo/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_delete_session)\n\n        msg = (\n            \"The request's session was deleted before the request completed. \"\n            \"The user may have logged out in a concurrent request, for example.\"\n        )\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(SessionInterrupted, msg):\n            # Handle the response through the middleware. It will try to save\n            # the deleted session which will cause an UpdateError that's caught\n            # and raised as a SessionInterrupted.\n            middleware(request)\n\n    def test_session_delete_on_end(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Before deleting, there has to be an existing cookie\n        request.COOKIES[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME] = \"abc\"\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # The cookie was deleted, not recreated.\n        # A deleted cookie header looks like:\n        #  \"Set-Cookie: sessionid=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; \"\n        #  \"Max-Age=0; Path=/\"\n        self.assertEqual(\n            'Set-Cookie: {}=\"\"; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; '\n            \"Max-Age=0; Path=/; SameSite={}\".format(\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,\n            ),\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n        # SessionMiddleware sets 'Vary: Cookie' to prevent the 'Set-Cookie'\n        # from being cached.\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n    @override_settings(\n        SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN=\".example.local\", SESSION_COOKIE_PATH=\"/example/\"\n    )\n    def test_session_delete_on_end_with_custom_domain_and_path(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Before deleting, there has to be an existing cookie\n        request.COOKIES[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME] = \"abc\"\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # The cookie was deleted, not recreated.\n        # A deleted cookie header with a custom domain and path looks like:\n        #  Set-Cookie: sessionid=; Domain=.example.local;\n        #              expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; Max-Age=0;\n        #              Path=/example/\n        self.assertEqual(\n            'Set-Cookie: {}=\"\"; Domain=.example.local; expires=Thu, '\n            \"01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT; Max-Age=0; Path=/example/; SameSite={}\".format(\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,\n                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,\n            ),\n            str(response.cookies[settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME]),\n        )\n\n    def test_flush_empty_without_session_cookie_doesnt_set_cookie(self):\n        def response_ending_session(request):\n            request.session.flush()\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_ending_session)\n\n        # Handle the response through the middleware\n        response = middleware(request)\n\n        # A cookie should not be set.\n        self.assertEqual(response.cookies, {})\n        # The session is accessed so \"Vary: Cookie\" should be set.\n        self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], \"Cookie\")\n\n    def test_empty_session_saved(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        If a session is emptied of data but still has a key, it should still\n        be updated.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def response_set_session(request):\n            # Set a session key and some data.\n            request.session[\"foo\"] = \"bar\"\n            return HttpResponse(\"Session test\")\n\n        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n        middleware = SessionMiddleware(response_set_session)\n\n        #
